# CSP Playground

> Browser-based Content Security Policy (CSP) header builder. Edit directives in a form, import from URLs or pasted headers, score policies heuristically, and copy server-specific export snippets. Policy editing runs in the browser; URL import uses a small lookup API (Vite middleware locally, Cloudflare Pages Functions in production).

Live site: https://csp-playground.dev
Repository: https://github.com/Nooshu/CSP-Playground

## What this project is

CSP Playground helps developers author correct Content Security Policy headers without memorizing syntax. It maps standard directives to accessible controls, explains sandbox flags, generates example nonces and style-attribute hashes, and exports ready-made config for common web servers.

## Key features

- Form editor for all standard CSP directives (fetch, document, navigation, reporting, Trusted Types, sandbox)
- Beginner, Intermediate, and Advanced presets (with confirmation before overwriting existing work)
- Import from URL (response headers or HTML meta tags) or paste headers/policy text
- Live security score with actionable recommendations
- Server export snippets: Apache, Nginx, Caddy, LiteSpeed, IIS, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, Firebase Hosting, Vercel, Traefik, Envoy
- HTML-only CSP option where the platform supports it; per-server setup notes (e.g. Cloudflare `_headers` vs `functions/_middleware.ts`)
- Enforce vs Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only toggle

## Tech stack

- TypeScript, vanilla DOM (no React), Vite
- Yarn v1 classic (`yarn.lock` is canonical; pin exact dependency versions)
- Vitest + jsdom tests; Playwright e2e smoke tests
- Biome for lint/format
- Deployed as static `dist/` on Cloudflare Pages with one Pages Function (`POST /api/csp-lookup`)

## Documentation for AI agents

- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/Nooshu/CSP-Playground/blob/main/AGENTS.md): conventions, commands, dependency policy
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](https://github.com/Nooshu/CSP-Playground/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): module boundaries and data flow
- [README.md](https://github.com/Nooshu/CSP-Playground/blob/main/README.md): user-facing features and getting started
